title: Zingy Green Pasta
summary: Green kale pasta is a well-loved vegetarian classic. This one comes with some zingy lemon and salty feta for a vibrant twist.

method.description: Bring a pot of salted water to the boil. Meanwhile, peel and finely dice the onion and discard any tough, thick kale stems.

Place a frying pan over a medium-high heat with a drizzle of olive oil. Once hot, add the onions and cook for 4-5 mins until softened and translucent. Add the kale to the pan and cook until wilted. Transfer to a blender with half the vegetable stock and all the olive oil. Let it cool down before blending until smooth, then pour it back into the pan.
Once the water reaches a boil, add the pasta and cook for 3 mins until al dente. Transfer the pasta to the sauce, reserving 250ml pasta water.

Set the pasta over a medium-high heat with the remaining vegetable stock. Toss together until hot, thinning it out with pasta water, as needed. Crumble in ½ of the feta and mix to combine. Taste and season with salt, pepper and lemon juice.
Share between bowls and top with lemon zest, walnuts and remaining crumbled feta.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
